papers_mined.title: Chairing of the Bard Wikipedia
papers_mined.note: Welsh eisteddfod bardic chair tradition since 1176; awarded to winning awdl in strict cynghanedd meter; new chair per eisteddfod; winners (Prifardd) become Archdruid eligible.

papers_mined.title: Cynghanedd Welsh Britannica
papers_mined.note: Strict alliteration + internal rhyme; 24 bardic metres; codified 1523 Caerwys Eisteddfod; obligatory in awdl.

papers_mined.title: Eisteddfod Wikipedia
papers_mined.note: Welsh festival of poetry chain; bards compete in strict metres; winner inherits bardic chair lineage.

papers_mined.title: Bardic Chairs Yr Ysgwrn
papers_mined.note: Each bardic chair commissioned per eisteddfod; tournament-style winner-takes-all chain succession.
